title: Power Factor Monitoring
spl:
index=power sourcetype="power_meter:pq"
| where pf < 0.92 OR thd_pct > 8
| timechart span=15m avg(pf) as avg_pf by feed_id
description: Low PF increases utility demand charges and heats conductors; correction banks need verification.
value: Low PF increases utility demand charges and heats conductors; correction banks need verification.
implementation: Poll main switchboard meters. Alert when PF drops below utility contract threshold. Correlate with capacitor bank status if monitored.
visualization: Line chart (PF trend), Gauge (current PF), Table (feeds out of spec).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Power meter SNMP (PQ meters).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="power_meter:pq"` (pf, thd, kw, kvar).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Poll main switchboard meters. Alert when PF drops below utility contract threshold. Correlate with capacitor bank status if monitored.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=power sourcetype="power_meter:pq"
| where pf < 0.92 OR thd_pct > 8
| timechart span=15m avg(pf) as avg_pf by feed_id
```

Understanding this SPL

**Power Factor Monitoring** — Low PF increases utility demand charges and heats conductors; correction banks need verification.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="power_meter:pq"` (pf, thd, kw, kvar). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Power meter SNMP (PQ meters). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: power; **sourcetype**: power_meter:pq. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=power, sourcetype="power_meter:pq". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where pf < 0.92 OR thd_pct > 8` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=15m** buckets with a separate series **by feed_id** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the PDU UI or SNMP poll for the same devices, readings, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (PF trend), Gauge (current PF), Table (feeds out of spec).
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
knownFalsePositives: Power spikes during equipment power cycling, scheduled load shifting, or DR drills.
